author: george_avs
content:
Hi folks! The rename of `SECONDS_PER_SLOT` to `SLOT_DURATION_MS` was recently merged (https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/4926).

I'm wondering — when can we expect clients to stop returning `SECONDS_PER_SLOT` in the Beacon API at `/eth/v1/config/spec`?

Lido has offchain apps that fetch this variable, including Oracle where any changes require a re-audit. It would be helpful to know the approximate timeline so we can plan accordingly 🙏

author: nflaig
content: I can speak for lodestar, we don't plan to remove this from `/eth/v1/config/spec` exactly for that reason, we don't wanna break anything. Maybe at some point it's worth to clean it up, good time would be when we reduce the slot duration because then you'd have to touch your setup anyways

author: potuz
content: cc <@884836500037066862> to track that we don't remove this either 🙂

author: jameshe5018
content: will add tracking for it, nothing done on it yet

author: tersec
content: as a general point, this should be in specs if so

author: tersec
content: having random actually-need-this is a good way to get silly folklore eth coding

author: tersec
content: and if it's not in specs, it should be fine to remove

author: tersec
content: i.e. I'd strongly suggest not relying on clients randomly deciding to to out-of-spec things

author: .paulharris
content:
We have to keep the fields for a fair while for compatibility with older client versions, otherwise things tend to break. could you try to be using the new fields by the time gloas drops? I think that'd be the earliest anyone would look at removing it...
We (teku) have a cleanup ticket for after gloas but if thats too soon we can note it...

author: potuz
content: I think it's fine to remove and there's no request to keep, the request was to give a timeline. It's also fine for us to keep optionally supporting this for a while

author: george_avs
content: Thanks for the quick responses! To clarify — i'm not asking to keep it forever, just wanted to understand whether it might disappear in the nearest client release, so we could decide if we need to rush hotfixes or can plan the changes for the next regular release cycle of our tooling. Sounds like we have time until Gloas, which is great — we'll migrate well before that

author: potuz
content: Yes this was our default behavior. Keep until Gloas, remove at the fork

author: potuz
content: We can keep for longer but removing unneeded code generates always a race within the team to write that PR
